Assembly: The assembly xgPatPell1.1 is based on 31x PacBio data, 10X Genomics Chromium data, and Arima Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project (https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with HiCanu, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, short-read polishing using FreeBayes-called variants from 10X Genomics Chromium reads aligned with LongRanger, and Hi-C based scaffolding with SALSA2. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHifi. Finally, the assembly was analysed and manually improved using gEVAL. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Chromosome 2 contains an large inversion between sister chromatids at approximately 15.43-87.97Mb. The inversion spans the majority of the chromosome 91.75Mb chromosome.

Assembly: The assembly ilLozFors1.hap1.1 is based on 52x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. In addition to the expected Z chromosome, which was identified through BUSCO gene painting aligned with ancestral Merian elements (https://doi.org/10.1038/s41559-024-02329-4), we have detected a triploid set of chromosome 29. Analysis of read coverage indicates that these chromosomes are genuine rather than artefacts resulting from assembly errors. However, to confirm their authenticity and better understand their origin and significance, additional sequencing of both male and female samples will be necessary.

Assembly: The assembly drRosCani1.hap1.1 is based on 90x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial and chloroplast genomes were assembled using OATK. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Sample is pentaploid (5n=35) and has unusual asymetric mieosis called 'canina meiosis' where 14 pairs of chromosomes recombine and the remaining 21 are inherited as univalents through the egg cell. Recombining pairs of chromosomes were identified based on sequence similarity (inferred from the HIC contact map). One set of 7 putatively recombining chromosomes was placed in the HAP1 (primary) assembly alongside the 21 univalents. The other set of 7 putatively recombining chromosomes have been placed in the HAP2 assembly. Recombining chromosomes are named 1 to 7 in size order with the remaining univalent chromosomeos named 8 - 21 in size order.
